title: TeamManagerAPI
description: Complete API contract for the Team Manager subsystem. Manages team graphs -- visual org charts of agents, humans, skills, and pipelines -- with deployment, scheduling, and AI generation capabilities. Phase 2 adds real subagent deployment orchestration, schedule governance, and generation cost telemetry.

X-Tenant-Id.description: Tenant identifier. Required on every request.

X-Workspace-Root.description: Absolute path to workspace root. Optional; defaults to server-configured workspace.

error_envelope.description: Standard error response envelope. Follows RFC 9457 spirit adapted to project conventions.

field.description: The specific field that caused a validation error, if applicable.

detail.description: Additional structured detail (e.g., cycle path for conflict errors).

node_kind.description: The functional type of a node in the team graph.

graph_summary.description: Compact graph representation returned in list operations.

graph_full.description: Full graph representation including all nodes and edges.

node.description: A node in the team graph representing a human, agent, skill, pipeline, group, context source, or annotation.

config.description: Kind-specific configuration. Schema varies by node kind.

agent_id.description: For kind=agent: references a sister/subagent config ID.

skill_id.description: For kind=skill: references a registered skill ID.

goal.description: For kind=agent or kind=pipeline: the goal or purpose.

pipeline_steps.description: For kind=pipeline: ordered list of node_ids forming the execution chain.

context_ref.description: For kind=context: reference to a memory key, document, or URL.

members.description: For kind=group: node_ids of group members.

style.description: Visual styling overrides for the node.

edge.description: A directed connection between two nodes in the team graph.

label.description: Optional display label on the edge.

style.description: Visual styling overrides for the edge.

token_usage.description: Token consumption metrics for an LLM operation.

deploy_node_status.description: Execution status for a single node within a deployment run. Tracks subagent lifecycle, progress, timing, and token usage.

status.description: Node execution state. 'queued' means the node is waiting for upstream dependencies. 'blocked' means an approval or resource wait.

subagent_run_id.description: The SubAgentProtocol run_id for this node. Null for non-agent nodes or if not yet spawned.

progress_pct.description: Execution progress percentage reported by the subagent. Null until the subagent reports progress.

model_id.description: The LLM model ID used for this node's execution. Reflects any per-node override applied.

runtime_ms.description: Wall-clock execution time in milliseconds. Populated on completion or failure.

token_usage.description: Token consumption for this node's subagent execution. Null until completed.

result_summary.description: Brief summary of the node's output on success.

artifacts.description: Structured output artifacts produced by this node's execution.

dependencies.description: Node IDs that must complete before this node can start (derived from graph topology).

deploy_run.description: A deployment run -- the result of deploying a team graph for execution. Tracks overall run state plus per-node subagent lifecycle, timing, and token usage.

status.description: 'partially_completed' indicates some nodes succeeded while others failed or were cancelled.

objective.description: The high-level objective for this deployment run. Used as the goal primer for root-level agents.

deploy_order.description: Topologically sorted node IDs representing the planned execution order.

node_statuses.description: Per-node execution status within the run.

progress_pct.description: Aggregate progress across all nodes. Computed as average of non-skipped node progress values.

runtime_ms.description: Total wall-clock time for the entire deployment run in milliseconds.

token_usage.description: Aggregate token usage across all nodes in the run.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Estimated total cost in USD based on model pricing and token usage. Null if not computable.

run_timeout_seconds.description: The timeout configured for this run.

schedule_id.description: If triggered by a schedule, the schedule_id that initiated this run.

cancelled_by.description: User ID that cancelled this run. Null if not cancelled or if cancelled by timeout.

events.description: Chronological event log for the run. Only populated when include_events=true on GET. Mirrors SubAgentProtocol event stream.

node_id.description: The node this event relates to, if applicable.

schedule.description: A recurring schedule for automatic graph deployment. Integrates with CronScheduler and governance pipeline.

user_id.description: The user who created/owns this schedule.

objective.description: The high-level objective for each deployment triggered by this schedule. Passed as the goal primer to root-level agents.

cron_expression.description: Standard cron expression (5-field) or shorthand (@hourly, @daily, */N * * * *). Parsed by croniter with fallback for common patterns.

timezone.description: IANA timezone for cron evaluation. Defaults to UTC.

requires_approval.description: When true, each scheduled deployment creates an approval_required item instead of executing immediately. Integrates with governance pipeline.

node_overrides.description: Per-node config overrides applied to every deployment triggered by this schedule. Keyed by node_id.

run_timeout_seconds.description: Max execution time for each deployment triggered by this schedule.

max_consecutive_failures.description: Auto-disable the schedule after this many consecutive run failures.

consecutive_failures.description: Current count of consecutive failed runs. Resets to 0 on success.

last_run_id.description: The run_id of the most recent deployment triggered by this schedule.

last_run_status.description: Status of the most recent run for quick reference without fetching the full run.

total_runs.description: Total number of deployments triggered by this schedule.

generate_result.description: Result of an AI generation request. Includes the mode-dependent payload plus telemetry for cost tracking.

result.description: Mode-dependent result payload. Exactly one of the mode-specific fields will be populated.

description.description: For mode=description: generated text description of the graph or node.

nodes.description: For mode=team_autofill: suggested nodes to add.

edges.description: For mode=team_autofill: suggested edges between the suggested nodes. Uses labels for cross-referencing since node IDs do not exist yet.

config.description: For mode=agent_autofill: suggested config fields for an agent node. Includes goal, model_id, done_when, and other agent-relevant settings.

tools.description: Suggested tool names for this agent.

reasoning.description: Brief explanation of the AI's reasoning behind the generated output. Aids human review of suggestions.

model_id.description: The LLM model used for generation.

telemetry.description: Cost and performance telemetry for the generation call.

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock time for the LLM generation call in milliseconds.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Estimated cost in USD based on model pricing. Null if pricing data is unavailable.

provider.description: The LLM provider that served the request (e.g., 'openrouter', 'anthropic', 'openai').

list_graphs.summary: List all team graphs for the tenant/workspace.

total.description: Total number of matching graphs (before limit/offset).

name.description: Display name for the graph. Must be unique per tenant.

tags.description: Freeform tags for organization and filtering.

response_400.description: Validation error (missing name, bad status, etc.).

response_409.description: Graph name already exists for this tenant.

get_graph.summary: Get a graph with all its nodes and edges.

update_graph.summary: Update graph metadata (name, description, status, tags). Does not modify nodes or edges.

name.description: New display name. Omit to keep current.

description.description: New description. Pass null to clear.

status.description: New status. Omit to keep current.

tags.description: Replace tags. Omit to keep current.

response_409.description: Name conflict with an existing graph.

delete_graph.summary: Delete a graph and all its nodes, edges, and schedules. Active deployments are cancelled.

response_403.description: Policy denied: graph has active deployments and force=false.

list_nodes.summary: List all nodes in a graph.

graph_id.description: The graph to list nodes from.

create_node.summary: Create a new node in a graph.

config.description: Kind-specific configuration. See node.$defs for per-kind fields.

update_node.summary: Update an existing node. Only provided fields are changed.

label.description: New label. Omit to keep current.

description.description: New description. Pass null to clear.

kind.description: Change node kind. Warning: may invalidate kind-specific config.

position.description: New canvas position. Omit to keep current.

config.description: Replace kind-specific config. Pass null to clear.

style.description: Replace visual style. Pass null to clear.

delete_node.summary: Delete a node. All connected edges are cascade-deleted.

edges_removed.description: Number of connected edges that were cascade-deleted.

list_edges.summary: List all edges in a graph.

create_edge.summary: Create a directed edge between two nodes.

target_node_id.description: The destination node. Must differ from source_node_id (no self-loops).

response_400.description: Validation error: self-loop, missing node references, etc.

response_404.description: Graph or referenced node not found.

response_409.description: Duplicate edge (same source, target, type) or cycle detected in pipeline edges.

deploy_graph.summary: Deploy a team graph for execution. Builds a topological deploy plan from the graph, then spawns SubAgentTask instances for each agent/pipeline node via SubAgentOrchestrator. Nodes execute in dependency order with real subagent workers.

objective.description: High-level objective for this deployment. Injected as goal primer into root-level agent system prompts. When omitted, each agent uses its node-level goal config.

session_id.description: Optional session context. A new session is created if omitted.

priority.description: Execution priority for all SubAgentTask instances in this run.

run_timeout_seconds.description: Max execution time for the entire deployment run. Individual nodes inherit this unless overridden.

node_overrides.description: Per-node config overrides keyed by node_id. Merged on top of the node's stored config. Supports model_id, goal, done_when, workspace_root, and run_timeout_seconds per node.

model_id.description: Override the LLM model for this specific node. Must be a valid OpenRouter or direct provider model ID.

goal.description: Override the goal for this specific node.

done_when.description: Override completion criteria for this node.

workspace_root.description: Override workspace root for this node's subagent.

run_timeout_seconds.description: Override timeout for this specific node.

dry_run.description: If true, validate the deployment plan and return the planned execution order, dependency graph, and resolved per-node configs without spawning any subagents.

response_200.description: Deployment initiated. Status will be 'pending' initially, transitioning to 'running' as subagents spawn. For dry_run=true, status will be 'pending' with populated deploy_order and node_statuses showing resolved configs.

blocked_nodes.description: Node IDs whose operations require governance approval.

response_202.description: Deployment requires governance approval. An approval item has been created.

response_400.description: Validation error: graph has no deployable nodes, invalid overrides, invalid model_id, etc.

response_403.description: Policy denied: deployment blocked by governance rules.

response_409.description: Graph already has an active deployment. Cancel the existing run first.

get_deploy_status.summary: Get the current status of a deployment run, including per-node progress, timing, and token usage. Optionally includes the full event log.

include_events.description: When true, the response includes the full chronological event log in the run.events field. Useful for detailed debugging but increases response size.

after_event_seq.description: When include_events=true, only return events with seq > this value. Enables incremental polling without re-fetching the full event log.

response_200.description: Current deployment run state. Poll this endpoint to track progress. The run.progress_pct field provides a quick aggregate. The run.node_statuses array provides per-node detail.

cancel_deploy.summary: Cancel a running deployment. Triggers cascade stop on all active subagent runs. Nodes that have already completed retain their results. In-progress nodes are terminated and marked as cancelled.

user_id.description: The user requesting cancellation. Recorded in cancelled_by.

reason.description: Optional reason for cancellation. Recorded in the event log.

nodes_cancelled.description: Number of in-progress or pending nodes that were cancelled.

nodes_completed.description: Number of nodes that had already completed before cancellation.

response_200.description: The run has been cancelled. Overall status will be 'cancelled' (or 'partially_completed' if some nodes finished successfully).

response_409.description: Run is already in a terminal state (completed, failed, or already cancelled). No action taken.

list_schedules.summary: List schedules, optionally filtered by graph_id. Returns schedule metadata including last run status and next run time.

graph_id.description: Filter schedules for a specific graph. When omitted, returns all schedules for the tenant.

total.description: Total number of matching schedules (before limit/offset).

create_schedule.summary: Create a recurring deployment schedule for a graph. Registers with CronScheduler and optionally gates each run through governance approval.

user_id.description: The user creating this schedule. Used for CronScheduler tenant+user scoping.

name.description: Optional human-readable name for the schedule.

objective.description: High-level objective passed to each deployment. Injected as the goal primer for root-level agents in the graph.

cron_expression.description: Standard 5-field cron expression or shorthand (@hourly, @daily, */N * * * *). Validated against croniter with fallback parsing.

enabled.description: Whether the schedule is immediately active.

requires_approval.description: When true, each triggered deployment creates an approval_required item instead of executing immediately. The deployment only proceeds after approval.

node_overrides.description: Per-node config overrides applied to every deployment triggered by this schedule. Keyed by node_id.

run_timeout_seconds.description: Max execution time for each triggered deployment.

max_consecutive_failures.description: Auto-disable the schedule after this many consecutive failures.

response_400.description: Invalid cron expression, missing required fields, or invalid timezone.

response_409.description: A schedule with the same graph_id and cron_expression already exists for this tenant.

update_schedule.summary: Update a schedule. Commonly used to toggle enabled/disabled, change the cron expression, update objective, or modify governance settings. Only provided fields are changed.

name.description: New name. Omit to keep current.

objective.description: New objective. Omit to keep current. Pass null to clear.

cron_expression.description: New cron expression. Omit to keep current. Recomputes next_run_at.

timezone.description: New timezone. Omit to keep current.

enabled.description: Enable or disable. Omit to keep current. Re-enabling resets consecutive_failures to 0.

requires_approval.description: Toggle governance approval gating. Omit to keep current.

node_overrides.description: Replace per-node config overrides. Pass null to clear all overrides.

run_timeout_seconds.description: New timeout. Omit to keep current.

max_consecutive_failures.description: New failure threshold. Omit to keep current.

response_400.description: Invalid cron expression, timezone, or field values.

delete_schedule.summary: Delete a schedule. Does not affect in-progress deployment runs.

generate.summary: AI-powered generation for team graphs. Supports three modes: description (generate a text summary of a graph or node), team_autofill (given an objective, generate a complete team structure with nodes and edges), and agent_autofill (given a role description, generate agent config including goal, model, and tools). All modes return token/cost telemetry.

mode.description: Generation mode. Each mode has different required fields and produces different result shapes.

graph_id.description: Required for mode=description (the graph to describe) and mode=agent_autofill (the graph containing the node). Optional for mode=team_autofill (used as context for generating team suggestions that complement existing nodes).

prompt.description: For mode=team_autofill: describe the team goal, purpose, or project requirements (REQUIRED). For mode=agent_autofill: describe the agent's intended role and responsibilities (REQUIRED). For mode=description: optional guidance for the description style/focus.

node_id.description: For mode=agent_autofill: the agent node to autofill config for (REQUIRED). For mode=description: optionally scope the description to a single node instead of the full graph.

model_id.description: Override the LLM model used for generation. Defaults to the runtime's configured model.

temperature.description: LLM temperature override. Lower values (0.0-0.3) produce more deterministic output; higher values (0.7-1.5) produce more creative suggestions. Defaults to 0.7 for team_autofill, 0.3 for others.

max_tokens.description: Max output tokens for the generation. Defaults to 2048.

apply_result.description: For mode=team_autofill: if true, automatically apply the suggested nodes and edges to the graph (requires graph_id). For mode=agent_autofill: if true, merge the suggested config into the node. For mode=description: if true, store the description in the graph/node metadata. When false (default), results are returned for human review only.

applied.description: True if apply_result was true and the results were successfully written to the graph. False if apply_result was false or if the write failed.

applied_node_ids.description: For mode=team_autofill with apply_result=true: the node IDs of newly created nodes, in the same order as generation.result.nodes.

applied_edge_ids.description: For mode=team_autofill with apply_result=true: the edge IDs of newly created edges.

response_400.description: Invalid mode, missing required fields for the chosen mode (e.g., graph_id for description, prompt for team_autofill), or invalid parameter values.

response_404.description: Referenced graph or node not found.

response_422.description: LLM returned unparseable output. The raw LLM response is included in detail.raw_response for debugging.

response_500.description: LLM generation failed due to provider error, timeout, or budget exhaustion.

response_503.description: LLM provider temporarily unavailable. Client should retry with exponential backoff.

error_taxonomy.description: Standard error types and their HTTP status codes.

validation_failed.description: Request body or query parameters fail schema validation. The 'field' property indicates the offending field when applicable.

not_found.description: The referenced resource (graph, node, edge, schedule, run) does not exist or does not belong to the requesting tenant.

policy_denied.description: The operation was blocked by tool governance policy. Typically returned when deploying graphs that contain high-risk agent configurations.

example.error: Deployment denied: graph contains voice_make_call agent which requires approval

approval_required.description: The operation requires human approval before proceeding. An approval item is created and its ID returned in the detail. The client should poll /approvals or present the approval to the user.

conflict.description: The operation conflicts with existing state. Common causes: duplicate graph name, duplicate edge, cycle detected in pipeline topology, active deployment already in progress.

internal_error.description: An unexpected server error occurred. The 'error' field contains a sanitized message. Full details are logged server-side.

generation_parse_error.description: The LLM returned output that could not be parsed into the expected structure. The raw response is provided for debugging.

example.error: Failed to parse LLM response as team structure

provider_unavailable.description: The LLM provider is temporarily unavailable. Retry with exponential backoff.

conventions.description: API-wide conventions for the Team Manager subsystem.
conventions.tenant_isolation: Every request must include tenant_id. Graphs, nodes, edges, schedules, and runs are scoped to a tenant. Cross-tenant access is never permitted.
conventions.workspace_context: workspace_root is optional. When provided, it scopes the operation to a specific workspace directory. When omitted, the server uses its default workspace.
conventions.identifiers: All resource IDs (graph_id, node_id, edge_id, schedule_id, run_id) are UUIDs generated server-side. Clients must not generate them.
conventions.timestamps: All timestamps are ISO 8601 in UTC (e.g., 2026-03-02T14:30:00Z). The server generates created_at and updated_at; clients cannot set them.
conventions.partial_updates: PUT endpoints accept partial payloads. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields retain their current values. Pass null explicitly to clear a nullable field.
conventions.cascade_deletes: Deleting a graph removes all its nodes, edges, and schedules. Deleting a node removes all connected edges. Deleting an edge or schedule is atomic.
conventions.deployment_lifecycle: Deploy creates a run (pending) -> transitions to running as SubAgentOrchestrator spawns workers for each node in topological order -> each node transitions through accepted/started/in_progress/completed|failed -> run completes or fails based on aggregate node outcomes. Cancel triggers cascade_stop on all active subagent runs. Poll GET /deploy/{run_id} for progress. Use include_events=true for the full event log.
conventions.scheduling_lifecycle: Schedules register with CronScheduler and fire deployments at cron intervals. When requires_approval=true, a governance approval item is created instead of immediate execution. After max_consecutive_failures, the schedule auto-disables. Re-enabling resets the failure counter.
conventions.generation_telemetry: All /generate responses include a telemetry block with token_usage, latency_ms, and estimated_cost_usd. This enables frontend cost tracking dashboards and budget enforcement.
conventions.provider_model_overrides: Agent nodes support model_id in their config for LLM provider selection. Deploy requests support per-node overrides via node_overrides. The resolution order is: node_override > node.config.model_id > runtime default (BOMBA_MODEL_ID).
conventions.content_type: All requests must send Content-Type: application/json. All responses return Content-Type: application/json.
conventions.idempotency: POST /api/team-manager/deploy accepts session_id for idempotent retries. POST /api/team-manager/deploy/{run_id}/cancel is idempotent -- cancelling an already-cancelled run returns the current state without error. Other POST endpoints are not idempotent; repeated calls create duplicate resources.
conventions.polling_pattern: For long-running deployments, poll GET /deploy/{run_id} at 1-5 second intervals. Use after_event_seq for incremental event retrieval to minimize payload size. The run.progress_pct field provides a quick aggregate progress indicator.
